channel.title: Pulse
channel.description: Curated podcast episodes by Pulse — current awareness radar. Episodes are organized by tracked topic.
channel.itunes:author: TARS
channel.itunes:summary: Curated podcast episodes by Pulse — current awareness radar. Episodes are organized by tracked topic.

item.title: [iran-war] — Iran Crisis Explodes: Allies Turn Away, Top Official Quits
item.description: A major shakeup hits Donald’s administration as National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent resigns warning there was no imminent threat from Iran. His statement, Donald’s response, and growing internal dissent, along with his Oval Office meeting with Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin, the GOP’s SAVE America Act push, and the subpoena of Attorney General Pam Bondi over the Epstein files are all examined. Mary Trump Live Tuesday March 17th! Please send comments/questions info@marytrumpme
item.itunes:author: The Mary Trump Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — Netanyahu Says “This Is What I Promised” For 40 Years & Iran Refuses Trump’s Offered Ceasefire
item.description: Welcome to The Daily Wrap Up, an in-depth investigatory show dedicated to bringing you the most relevant independent news, as we see it, from the last 24 hours (3/1/26).\nAs always, take the information discussed in the video below and research it for yo
item.itunes:author: The Last American Vagabond

item.title: [iran-war] — Netanyahu Says “This Is What I Promised” For 40 Years & Iran Refuses Trump’s Offered Ceasefire
item.description: Welcome to The Daily Wrap Up, an in-depth investigatory show dedicated to bringing you the most relevant independent news, as we see it, from the last 24 hours (3/1/26).\nAs always, take the information discussed in the video below and research it for yo
item.itunes:author: The Last American Vagabond

item.title: [openclaw] — The AI Agent That Thinks While You Sleep
item.description: This week on Open Claw Cast: OpenClaw drops a massive secrets security overhaul, Claude 4.6 gets adaptive thinking by default, and a community builder gives his AI agent a literal subconscious that runs overnight for -3/month. We also dig into the bru
item.itunes:author: OpenClaw Cast

item.title: [openclaw] — Episode 6: The v2026.2.24 Update & Bot Social Networks
item.description: Episode 6 covers the massive OpenClaw v2026.2.24 release with its new 5-tab Android shell and security hardening, the v2026.2.23 SSRF policy shift, the origins of the Molty mascot and the Lobster Way culture, Moltbook — a social network built by bots
item.itunes:author: OpenClaw Daily

item.title: [openclaw] — #491 – OpenClaw: The Viral AI Agent that Broke the Internet – Peter Steinberger
item.description: Nathan Lambert and Sebastian Raschka are machine learning researchers, engineers, and educators. Nathan is the post-training lead at the Allen Institute for AI (Ai2) and the author of The RLHF Book. Sebastian Raschka is the author of Build a Large Langu
item.itunes:author: Lex Fridman Podcast

item.title: [openclaw] — OpenClaw: The Ultimate Rapid Prototyping Machine for the AI Era - #115
item.description: OpenClaw is not just another AI tool — it’s a fundamental shift in how companies build, automate, and operate.\nIn this episode, Malcolm Werchota explains why we are entering the era of multi-agent systems and how OpenClaw enables businesses to proto
item.itunes:author: The AI Cookbook: AI Tools | Enterprise AI | Leadership

item.title: [openclaw] — Episode 9: OpenClaw v2026.3.1 — When Your Assistant Starts Acting Like Infrastructure
item.description: Episode 9 of OpenClaw Daily covers OpenClaw v2026.3.1 — a reliability and infrastructure release: Discord thread session lifecycles, Telegram DM topics, Android node notification actions + device health, health/readiness probes, WebSocket-first stream
item.itunes:author: OpenClaw Daily

item.title: [openclaw] — Special: Building a Distributed AI Cluster with exo-labs
item.description: A deep-dive special episode on building a distributed AI inference cluster using exo-labs and Apple Silicon. Nova and Alloy cover everything from installation and RDMA networking to model selection, daemonization, and an honest verdict on who should act
item.itunes:author: OpenClaw Daily

item.title: [iran-war] — Escalations in the Conflict Between Israel and Iran, Steven Nekhaila on U.S. Global Strategy, No Kings Update & Rep. Nick LaLota Takes on Dark Money Attacks
item.description:
Tonight's rundown: Hey BillOReilly.com Premium and Concierge Members, welcome to the No Spin News for Monday, June 16, 2025. Stand Up for Your Country. 


 Talking Points Memo: Bill takes a closer look at the ongoing Iran-Israel conflict.

 Ste
item.itunes:author: Bill O’Reilly’s No Spin News and Analysis

item.title: [openclaw] — Episode 10: The Document & Memory Revolution (OpenClaw Daily, 32min)
item.description: OpenClaw March 3, 2026 release: PDF analysis tool with native model support, Ollama memory embeddings for full local memory stacks, SecretRef expansion to 64 targets, sessions attachments for inter-agent file passing, Telegram streaming defaults, MiniMa
item.itunes:author: OpenClaw Daily

item.title: [openclaw] — OpenClaw: The Ultimate Rapid Prototyping Machine for the AI Era - #115
item.description: OpenClaw is not just another AI tool — it’s a fundamental shift in how companies build, automate, and operate.\nIn this episode, Malcolm Werchota explains why we are entering the era of multi-agent systems and how OpenClaw enables businesses to proto

item.title: [iran-war] — JSE, Bidvest release strong results and U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran disrupts global shipping
item.description: Stephen Grootes speaks to JSE CEO Leila Fourie and Bidvest CEO Mpumi Madisa about their latest performance results, unpacking revenue growth, strategic priorities and what lies ahead for their respective businesses.\n In other interviews, Timothy Walker,
item.itunes:author: The Money Show

item.title: [iran-war] — Are Israel and the US Trying To Start a Civil War in Iran?
item.description: In the wake of the US's assassination of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, many are asking – will Iran actually see regime change, as President Donald Trump has called for? What would that take? And is the real goal to engineer a civil w
item.itunes:author: Mehdi Unfiltered

item.title: [iran-war] — The Iran War: British Uncertainty vs. French Resolve
item.description: In this Conflicted Conversation, Thomas speaks to two friends of the show about how Europe is responding to the war with Iran. In the first half, the Rt Hon Tom Tugendhat MP — former chair of the UK Foreign Affairs Select Committee and former UK Secur
item.itunes:author: CONFLICTED

item.title: [iran-war] — How the Iran–US Conflict Could Impact Australian Property, Inflation and Interest Rates
item.description: We talk to economist Saul Eslake from Corinna Economic Advisory about how the ripple effects of the Iran war could reach Australia’s economy. Higher petrol and food costs may squeeze household budgets and consumer confidence, while inflation risks cou
item.itunes:author: Aussie Real Estate Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — News - Iran War Widens, Afghanistan–Pakistan Fighting, New U.S. Operation in Ecuador
item.description: We don’t have whatever they were giving JFK to power through the Cuban Missile Crisis, but we’re keeping up here. This week’s news: in the Iran War, the U.S. prepares to use Kurdish proxy forces against the Islamic Republic (1:26) while offering s
item.itunes:author: American Prestige

item.title: [iran-war] — U.S. Striking “Deeper” Into Iran As War Spreads Across Mideast
item.description: Kasie Hunt talks to the panel as the U.S. says it will strike ‘deeper’ into Iran. Rep. Pat Ryan reacts after Secretary Hegseth suggested that prominent media coverage of service member casualties is meant to “make the president look bad." The Stat
item.itunes:author: The Arena with Kasie Hunt

item.title: [iran-war] — Hegseth: U.S. “Just Getting Started” As Iran War Spreads
item.description: As America’s war widens, the administration ditches the speak softly while touting the big stick. Plus, a secret gamble. The US seeks to trigger an uprising in Iran. \nLearn more about your ad choices. Visit podcastchoices.com/adchoices
item.itunes:author: CNN NewsNight with Abby Phillip

item.description: In this 136th episode of the On Investors' Minds - APAC Edition podcast, Tai Hui provides an initial assessment of the impact post the U.S. and Israel air strikes on Iran (and its retaliatory actions) during the weekend of February 28th. What people

item.itunes:author: On Investors’ Minds - APAC Edition

item.title: [iran-war] — 03.03.26 - Iran conflict: investment strategies for uncertainty
item.description: The headlines are moving fast, but this Market Sense special report will tackle the escalating conflict in Iran and what this new geopolitical risk could mean for the markets and the global economy. Our Fidelity leaders discuss the impact on oil prices
item.itunes:author: Fidelity Viewpoints: Market Sense

item.title: [iran-war] — War Powers and the Road to Iran
item.description: As the White House signals openness to escalation and murky and conflicting objectives, uncertainty clouds both the legal basis and strategic endgame of U.S. involvement in Iran. The Cato Institute's Justin Logan, Thomas Berry, and Brandan P. Buck exam
item.itunes:author: Cato Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — Trump Predicts a Multi-Week War as Badly Wounded Iran Strikes Out at Neighbors, Conflict Widens
item.description:
Tuesday, 3/3/26

TODAY'S TOPICS:
How is the operation going?
Biggest operational risks for America and Israel.
Biggest political risks for Team Trump.
How is the media doing?
MAGA and the conflict.
Congressional Democrats and the conflict.
’28 Democra
item.itunes:author: The Morning Meeting

item.title: [openclaw] — How OpenClaw's Creator Uses AI to Run His Life in 40 Minutes | Peter Steinberger
item.description: Peter is the creator of OpenClaw (formerly Molt - the name keeps changing 😅), the hottest AI right now with 2M visitors in a week. In our interview, Peter shared is personal favorite use cases including using Claw to check in to flights, control hi
item.itunes:author: Behind the Craft

item.description: Segment 1: Interview with Mathias Katz What if you had enterprise-grade network security protections traveling with your users' laptops? What if it could be built into the laptop, but still stay safe even if the laptop OS and firmware were entirely comp

item.title: [iran-war] — The Red Sea in the eye of a geopolitical storm: proxy wars, maritime strategy and trade
item.description: The Red Sea has become the centre of a geopolitical crisis. How to secure one of the world’s most crowded trade routes? With, amongst others, NATO-Chief of Staff Geoffrey van Leeuwen we speak about proxy wars, maritime strategy and trade.\nEvery year,
item.itunes:author: De Balie

item.description: Tehran’s nuclear-weapons race was a risk the U.S., Israel and others couldn’t tolerate, and its terrorism and brutality have only worsened. Its development of weapons that could reach Europe is a growing, grave threat. But as more than one of this e
item.itunes:author: Full Comment

item.description: In this episode of STRAT, Retired Marine Intelligence Officer Hal Kempfer dissects the recent ceasefire between Israel and Iran, exploring its implications and the dynamics that led to this temporary halt in hostilities. We examine Iran's missile respon
item.itunes:author: STRAT

item.title: [iran-war] — Stocks Stage Comeback Amid U.S.-Iran Conflict… And Jamie Dimon’s Credit Concerns 3/2/26
item.description: Stocks reversing midday as investors bought the dip after the U.S.-Iran attacks. How the energy market is responding, and what the conflict will mean for markets. Plus, What JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon had to say about the recent private credit crunch, the
item.itunes:author: CNBC's "Fast Money"

item.title: [iran-war] — Conflict spreads to the wider region after the US-Israel strikes on Iran
item.description: The US and Israel’s campaign against Iran, that resulted in the death of Tehran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei, has now expanded into a regional conflict. Monocle’s Gulf correspondent Inzamam Rashid brings us the latest as strikes have now been seen in
item.itunes:author: The Globalist

item.title: [iran-war] — 297. Operation Ajax: The 1953 CIA Plot to Topple Iran (and Other Background Notes)
item.description: Is Iran at an historic turning point? Iranian-born Alex Vatanka of the Middle East Institute weighs in on the likely paths and consequences. We also discuss the US history of intervention in Iran dating back to the CIA’s Operation Ajax in 1953.\nOrder
item.itunes:author: The Sharyl Attkisson Podcast

item.description:
Note that this conversation took place before Hamas addressed some conditions of President Donald Trump’s proposed peace plan and said it agreed to release all remaining hostages.



This was the most requested conversation I've ever had, and one
item.itunes:author: Conversations with Coleman

item.title: [iran-war] — Global Hotspots: U.S. Weighs Direct Involvement in Israel/Iran Conflict
item.description: This week, Israel escalated its military campaign against Iran’s nuclear and military infrastructure, while the U.S. weighed potential involvement in the conflict. In the Indo-Pacific, Taiwan added Chinese companies to its export blacklist, limiting t
item.itunes:author: New Lines Institute for Strategy and Policy | Podcasts

item.title: [iran-war] — Gulf states caught in the middle of US-Iran conflict
item.description: Investors turned to gold and the US dollar in the wake of the Iran conflict, and Gulf states are panicking as Iran targets them in retaliatory strikes. Plus,can the US economy handle the surging oil prices caused by the attacks? \nMentioned in this podc
item.itunes:author: FT News Briefing

item.description: Dive deep into the Israel-Iran conflict with geopolitical experts Jason Baidya and Deepak Gyawali in this comprehensive podcast episode. Explore the complex dynamics of the Middle East unrest, including the recent escalation, US aid, and the strategic r
item.itunes:author: Sushant Pradhan Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — Wargaming WW3: how and where the next global conflict could actually unfold
item.description: From Taiwan to Estonia and Latvia, the prospect of World War Three feels closer than ever - that is unless you're one of those people who thinks it's already begun.\nPeter Apps, Reuters' Global Defence Commentator, is not one of those people, but he does
item.itunes:author: Iran: The Latest

item.title: [iran-war] — EI Weekly Listen — Kimberly Kagan on the United States and the new way of war
item.description: The United States, still the dominant military power in the world, is immersed in a new era of warfare that it has not yet recognised as endemic and enduring. America is losing its wars to less powerful but more adaptable adversaries, while preparing inadequately for future inter-state conflicts. Re
item.itunes:author: The EI Podcast

item.title: [iran-war] — U.S.-Iran Conflict, Part 2: The Iranian model of asymmetric warfare
item.description: A big part of America's fear of Iran arises from the idea that Iran sponsors various militant groups in the region, Hezbollah being a prominent example.

item.description: Features national security and White House correspondents breaking down the shifting administration goals and the emerging domestic political friction over launching a regime-change war without congressional approval.

item.title: [iran-war] — A New Era of Conflict - Understanding the U.S. Strategy Against Iran and Its Consequences
item.description: Provides a deep-dive analysis into the specific military strategy behind the U.S. strikes, offering a hawkish expert perspective on why a protracted, multi-domain campaign is viewed as a necessary strategic reset despite international backlash.

item.itunes:author: Mo News

item.title: [iran-war] — The Other Iran-Israel War
item.description: In addition to the three-dimensional war of bombs and missiles, Iran and Israel—and the US—are squaring off in a cyber battle. With everything from water infrastructure to banking connected to the internet in some way, how much damage can cyberattacks do? \nGuest: Maggie Miller, POLITICO cybersecuri
item.itunes:author: What Next | Daily News and Analysis

item.title: [iran-war] — Is the U.S. headed toward military conflict with Iran?
item.description:
New York Times journalist David Sanger discusses how we got here, the state of Iran's nuclear weapons program, the likelihood of U.S. military force against Iran and if Trump's goal is regime change.

To manage podcast ad preferences, review the links below:

See pcm.adswizz.com for information abou
item.itunes:author: Fresh Air

item.title: [iran-war] — Experts Feared a Wider War. It's Finally Here
item.description: We’re in a “history defining moment that will shape the Middle East for years to come.”
item.itunes:author: What Next | Daily News and Analysis

item.title: [OpenClaw] — Is OpenClaw’s Hype Real? Are AI Agents Replacing Jobs? What’s the Anti-AI Thesis?
item.description: The Napkin Math duo is back with another episode. We spend most of this one trying to make sense of AI agents, OpenClaw - the new kid on the block, and why this wave feels messier but more real than the last few. We talk through hype vs reality, what agents can actually do today, and how work is quietly shifting from roles and seats to tasks and systems. We also discuss AI fluency levels, how people are using Claude Code in real marketing workflows, one-person companies, and end with the Anti-AI portfolio. Timestamps 00:00 Intro 01:15 OpenClaw & the agent breakout moment 06:52 Hype vs. reality: shiny tools, boring winners 09:45 The Dropbox lesson: abstraction beats raw tech 12:43 AI agents, security & where this breaks 19:07 AI vs SaaS, leverage & outcome-based work 24:58 Are AI agents a
item.itunes:author: Napkin Math

item.title: [OpenClaw] — Moltbook 3:16 – DTNS Live 5113
item.description:
We share our feelings about Moltbook, a Reddit-like site for AI agents from popular AI assistant platform OpenClaw. Komei asked what steps any of us have taken to make provisions for our accounts after we die. And the Information reports Nvidia will not be introducing any new GPUs in 2026. Is this the final nail in the coffin for the DIY PC enthusiast hobby?
Starring Sarah Lane, Tom Merritt, Robb Dunewood, Len Peralta, Roger Chang, Joe.
To read the show notes click here!
Support the show on Patreon by becoming a supporter!
item.itunes:author: Daily Tech News Show (VIDEO)

item.title: [OpenClaw] — OpenClaw & Moltbook: AI Agents and Cybersecurity Risks
item.description:
Autonomous AI assistants are hitting the mainstream — but at what cost? This week, we discuss the recent OpenClaw phenomenon (formerly Clawdbot/Moltbot), the security fiasco surrounding Moltbook’s exposed database, and the quirky yet concerning AI agent dating platform MoltMatch. We explore the privacy and cybersecurity implications of entrusting AI agents with sensitive access and how defenders should think about emerging agentic risks.
** Links mentioned on the show **
OpenClaw (a.k.a. Moltbot) is everywhere all at once, and a disaster waiting to happen
https://garymarcus.substack.com/p/openclaw-aka-moltbot-is-everywhere
Exposed Moltbook Database Let Anyone Take Control of Any AI Agent on the Site
https://www.404media.co/exposed-moltbook-database-let-anyone-take-control-of-any-ai-
item.itunes:author: Shared Security Podcast

item.title: [Iran-US Conflict] — BONUS: What does the Iran conflict mean for the global economy?
item.description: Roughly a quarter of the world's oil supply is at risk as the biggest military strike in decades unfolds in the Middle East. Rory Johnston is a geopolitical analyst who writes the Substack Commodity Context. He talks to Paul Haavardsrud about how this conflict play out for Canadian crude and the global economy.
item.itunes:author: Cost of Living

item.title: [Iran-US Conflict] — What Is a War?
item.description:
The Trump administration’s attacks on Venezuelan boats defy even the broadest interpretation of the president's war powers.
Hosted by Simplecast, an AdsWizz company. See pcm.adswizz.com for information about our collection and use of personal data for advertising.

item.itunes:author: drstorm.substack.com

item.title: [openclaw] — [openclaw] The OpenClaw-ification of AI - The AI Daily Brief
item.itunes:author: The AI Daily Brief

item.title: [Israel-US vs Iran] — Iran’s leadership, retaliation, travel and oil disruption and Trump’s plan
item.description:
Israel has launched a new wave of strikes on Tehran after killing Iran's supreme leader and leaving the Islamic Republic grappling to rebuild its leadership. The U.S. and Israeli strikes, and Iranian retaliation, have sent shockwaves through sectors from shipping to air travel to oil. Inside Iran, some grieved for Khamenei while others have celebrated his death. Protests against the military action have also erupted across Pakistan and Iraq.
Follow the ⁠latest developments in Iran live⁠ by subs
item.itunes:author: Reuters World News

item.title: [Israel-US vs Iran] — Breaking news podcast: Inside the high-stakes U.S.-Israel attack on Iran | Haaretz defense analyst Amos Harel
item.description: In this special edition of the Haaretz Podcast, recorded during the first hours of the dramatic joint U.S.-Israeli attack on Iran, and as Tehran began its retaliatory strikes on Israel and on U.S. targets across the Middle East, Haaretz senior analyst Amos Harel joins host Allison Kaplan Sommer for a real-time update and discussion. "The stakes are much higher than last time," Harel said, referring to the 12 day Israel-Iran war in June 2025. For Israelis, "there is a certain amount of dange
item.itunes:author: Haaretz
